head.title: HTML5 Audio
head.summary: Embedding sound files with SilkPage XML

webpage.para: SilkPage now supports a native audio element in page content. It renders as the HTML5 audio element so a page can expose browser-native playback controls without dropping into namespaced XHTML markup.

section.title: Usage
section.para: Use the audio element directly in a SilkPage page. The usual HTML5 attributes such as controls, preload, autoplay, loop and muted are passed through unchanged.
section.audio: Your browser does not support HTML5 audio.
section.html:pre: <audio src="media/intro.mp3" controls="controls" preload="metadata"> Your browser does not support HTML5 audio. </audio>

section.title: Multiple Sources
section.para: For broader browser compatibility, nest one or more source elements and let the browser choose the first supported format.
section.html:pre: <audio controls="controls" preload="metadata"> <source src="media/intro.ogg" type="audio/ogg"/> <source src="media/intro.mp3" type="audio/mpeg"/> Your browser does not support HTML5 audio. </audio>

section.title: Tracks And Metadata
section.para: SilkPage also passes through nested track elements, so timed text and metadata tracks can be attached when needed.
section.html:pre: <audio controls="controls"> <source src="media/interview.mp3" type="audio/mpeg"/> <track kind="captions" src="media/interview.en.vtt" srclang="en" label="English"/> </audio>

section.title: Regression Test
section.para: The repository includes test-case-01 as a regression check for this feature. Run make test-case-01 from the project root to validate the XML source, rebuild the website, and confirm that the generated page contains the expected HTML5 audio, source, and track markup.
